channel.title: OnOptions
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: High flyer: Palantir CEO Alex Karp spent $17.2 million on private jets in 2025, filing reveals
item.description: One out of every 3.5 days was spent in the air for the Palantir CEO during 2025, filing shows.

item.title: Mark Cuban slams the NBA for focusing on tanking and ignoring the real issue for fans: ‘It should worry more about pricing fans out of games’
item.description: The Dallas Mavericks, with Cuban as a stakeholder, raised prices nearly 9% ahead of the 2025-2026 season, despite fans’ ire over trading All-Star Luka...

item.title: Klarna CEO says firm will likely cut 1,000 employees by 2030—partially due to AI
item.description: Sebastian Siemiatkowski, CEO of Swedish fintech company Klarna, says the organization is set to drastically downsize. And he says he shares his outloo...

item.title: Bayer proposes $7.25 billion Roundup settlement as Supreme Court case looms
item.description: A new $7.25 billion settlement between Bayer and a group of cancer patients could wrap up a huge wave of lawsuits against the company over allegations...

item.title: Everyone Hates Meetings, So Why Do They Keep Multiplying? RTO Is One Culprit
item.description: Studies show some employees are spending more than 10 hours per week in meetings.

item.title: OpenAI’s Latest Moves Speak Volumes About the Future of AI Agents
item.description: OpenAI recently made a key hire—and is bringing a startup called Nerve on board, too. Here’s why.

item.title: Big Tech execs playing ‘Russian roulette’ in the AI arms race could risk human extinction, warns top researcher
item.description: Allowing companies free rein on AI is bringing on a new level of existential risk, Stuart Russell argues.

item.title: OpenAI, Google, and Perplexity near approval to host AI directly for the U.S. government (exclusive)
item.description: Three AI companies—OpenAI, Google, and Perplexity—are on the verge of receiving approval to sell their technology, hosted on their own cloud systems, ...

item.title: Zuckerberg testifies at social media addiction trial
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg and opposing lawyers dueled in a Los Angeles courtroom on Wednesday, where the Meta CEO answered questions about young people’s use of...

item.title: Digital excellence can yield exceptional in-person experiences
item.description: When I cofounded Brilliant Earth in 2005, e-commerce was still in its infancy. I believed technology could reshape the jewelry industry entirely—chang...

item.title: New Tax Rules Are in Effect This Season—and Many Filers Don’t Know About Them
item.description: New deductions and rule changes could mean a larger refund for millions of taxpayers this year.

item.title: A New GLP-1 Drug Being Developed May Come With a Surprising New Safety Concern
item.description: Drug maker Eli Lilly is developing a new, more powerful weight-loss drug. Does it work too well?

item.title: Buffalo Wild Wings wins legal debate after a customer discovered his boneless wings contained no real wing meat at all
item.description: A judge ruled in favor of Buffalo Wild Wings for a March 2023 case, saying the customer’s argument “has no meat on its bones.”

item.title: Jake Paul says a chance meeting with Sam Altman at Trump’s inauguration led to an OpenAI investment and a crash course in ruthless 15-minute meetings
item.description: Paul said the OpenAI CEO showed him “time is the most valuable thing.”

item.title: Warren Buffett’s Final Berkshire Bet Revealed in New Filing—It’s a Business He Once Called ‘Toast’
item.description: After years of exiting newspapers, Berkshire Hathaway quietly returned to one.
